evals.name: generic-audit-invocation
evals.expected_output: Assistant runs Phase 1 (static analysis, skipping missing tools), spawns five parallel subagents in Phase 2 with the canonical scopes (resource lifecycle, data integrity, concurrency, error paths, external boundaries), and writes docs/audits/audit-YYYY-MM-DD.md with the required sections. Summary line includes a severity tally that matches the findings list exactly.

evals.name: pre-release-robustness-check
evals.prompt: before we cut the next release I want a robustness review — races, leaks, swallowed exceptions, the lot
evals.expected_output: Assistant recognises this as an audit trigger, runs the full three-phase workflow, and the output report emphasises error paths + concurrency findings. Suggested next-steps section orders branches by severity (critical first). Does not suggest fixes inline — the report is a defect list.

evals.name: refuses-to-fabricate-when-tools-missing
evals.prompt: audit the codebase. assume bandit etc aren't installed.
evals.expected_output: Phase 1 reports all four tools as skipped in the 'Skipped' section with honest reason ('not on PATH / not importable'). Does NOT fabricate static-analysis findings. Phase 2 still runs all five subagents. Phase 3 report's Summary line says 'Phase 1 skipped (tools missing); Phase 2 ran all five subagents'.

evals.name: date-collision-does-not-overwrite
evals.prompt: audit again — yes I know we audited this morning.
evals.expected_output: Assistant notices docs/audits/audit-YYYY-MM-DD.md already exists and writes audit-YYYY-MM-DD-v2.md instead of overwriting. First run's findings remain on disk as a historical record.

evals.name: style-review-not-this-skill
evals.prompt: can you clean up the code style and make it more readable?
evals.expected_output: Assistant does NOT invoke audit — recognises this as a style / readability request and either declines the audit skill or proposes a simpler review pass. The skill description's 'When NOT to invoke' section kicks in.

evals.name: non-python-repo-phase-1-degrades-gracefully
evals.expected_output: On a repo containing only Cargo.toml and .rs files (Rust), Phase 1 detects Rust, tries `cargo clippy` and `cargo audit` (and `cargo-audit` alias variants), skips tools not installed with honest reasons, and does NOT run Python tools. Phase 2 still spawns all five subagents with the same scopes. Phase 3 report records the detected language in the Summary section.

evals.name: severity-tally-matches-findings
evals.expected_output: The Summary line 'Total findings: N (critical: N · high: N · medium: N · low: N)' must sum correctly to N, and each severity count must equal the number of findings tagged [critical], [high], etc. in the body. Assistant recounts before writing the summary. If a later edit strikes out a false positive, the tally re-counts strikethrough lines as audit-trail but does NOT include them in the headline count.
